title: Implement Hook System for Tool and Agent Lifecycle Events
description:
Add support for hooks similar to Python SDK - PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SubagentStop, UserPromptSubmit, Stop, PreCompact. Should include HookCallback type, HookMatcher with patterns, and async hook execution.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:

Hook Events (6 types):
- PreToolUse: Before tool execution (can modify input, control permissions)
- PostToolUse: After tool execution (add context to response)
- UserPromptSubmit: When user submits prompt
- Stop: When execution stops
- SubagentStop: When subagent completes
- PreCompact: Before compacting conversation history

HookCallback Signature:
type HookCallback func(ctx context.Context, input HookInput, toolUseID *string, context HookContext) (map[string]any, error)

HookMatcher Structure:
- Matcher *string (pattern like "Bash|Edit|Write")
- Hooks []HookCallback
- Timeout *float64 (default 60s)

HookInput (discriminated union via hook_event_name):
- Base: SessionID, TranscriptPath, Cwd, PermissionMode
- PreToolUse: +ToolName, ToolInput
- PostToolUse: +ToolName, ToolInput, ToolResponse
- UserPromptSubmit: +Prompt
- Stop/SubagentStop: +StopHookActive
- PreCompact: +Trigger, CustomInstructions

HookOutput (JSON):
- Control: continue, suppressOutput, stopReason
- Decision: decision, systemMessage, reason
- HookSpecificOutput (event-dependent)
- Async: async, asyncTimeout

Registration: During Initialize(), assign callback IDs, store in map

title: Enable Dynamic Model Switching Mid-Conversation
description:
Implement set_model() equivalent to allow switching between models during a conversation via control protocol.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:

API Signature:
func (c *Client) SetModel(ctx context.Context, model *string) error
- nil = use default model
- non-nil = specific model ID

Supported Models (full model IDs):
- "claude-sonnet-4-5"
- "claude-opus-4-1-20250805"
- "claude-3-5-haiku-20241022"
- Any string the CLI recognizes (server validates)

Control Protocol:
- Request: {"subtype": "set_model", "model": "..."}
- Response: success or error via control_response
- 60s timeout for request/response

Timing Requirements:
- Between turns ONLY (not mid-response)
- Pattern: Query() → Receive() → SetModel() → Query()
- Cannot switch while Claude is generating

State Preservation:
- Conversation context fully preserved
- Previous messages remain in history
- Only future responses use new model
- Session ID and permissions unchanged

Constraints:
- Streaming mode only (requires bidirectional client)
- Must be connected (call Connect() first)
- No validation in SDK (CLI validates model names)

Use Case: Switch to haiku for analysis, opus for implementation

title: Expose Control Protocol for Bidirectional SDK Communication
description:
Parse and route control protocol messages (SDKControlRequest/Response) to SDK consumers. Enable hook callbacks, permission requests, and dynamic configuration changes.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:

Message Types:
- SDKControlRequest: type, request_id, request (union of subtypes)
- SDKControlResponse: type, response (success/error with request_id)

Request Subtypes:
1. initialize - Register hooks/callbacks with unique IDs
2. can_use_tool - Permission callback (tool_name, input, suggestions)
3. hook_callback - Hook execution (callback_id, input, tool_use_id)
4. set_permission_mode - Change permissions runtime
5. set_model - Switch models mid-conversation
6. interrupt - Cancel operations
7. mcp_message - MCP server routing

Protocol Flow:
- Bidirectional SDK ↔ CLI communication
- Request ID format: req_{counter}_{4-byte-hex-random}
- 60s default timeout for requests
- Concurrent requests via request_id matching

Go Types Needed:
- type SDKControlRequest struct { Type, RequestID, Request }
- type ControlRequest interface{} (discriminated union)
- type SDKControlResponse struct { Type, Response }
- PendingControlResponses map[string]chan interface{}

Integration: Transport interface, message routing in ReceiveMessages loop

title: Add Tool Permission Callback Support (can_use_tool)
description:
Implement CanUseTool callback that allows dynamic permission decisions before tool execution. Should support PermissionResultAllow/Deny and permission suggestions.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:

Callback Signature:
type CanUseTool func(ctx context.Context, toolName string, input map[string]any, context ToolPermissionContext) (PermissionResult, error)

PermissionResult Types:
- PermissionResultAllow:
  - Behavior: "allow"
  - UpdatedInput: map[string]any (optional modified input)
  - UpdatedPermissions: []PermissionUpdate (optional new rules)
  
- PermissionResultDeny:
  - Behavior: "deny"
  - Message: string (reason for denial)
  - Interrupt: bool (stop conversation)

ToolPermissionContext:
- Signal: any (reserved for future abort signal)
- Suggestions: []PermissionUpdate (from CLI)

PermissionUpdate Types:
- addRules, replaceRules, removeRules
- setMode, addDirectories, removeDirectories
- Includes: rules, behavior, mode, directories, destination

Integration:
- Triggered via control protocol can_use_tool request
- Streaming mode only (auto-sets permission_prompt_tool_name="stdio")
- Mutually exclusive with permission_prompt_tool_name option
- No callback timeout (uses 60s control protocol timeout)

Use Cases: Block dangerous commands, deny system dir writes, modify inputs for safety

Reference: /tmp/claude-agent-sdk-python/claude_agent_sdk/types.py lines 135-157, examples/tool_permission_callback.py

title: Enable Dynamic Permission Mode Changes
description:
Implement set_permission_mode() to change permission settings during execution without restarting.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:

API Signature:
func (c *Client) SetPermissionMode(ctx context.Context, mode string) error

Permission Modes:
- "default": CLI prompts for dangerous tools (interactive)
- "acceptEdits": Auto-accept file edit/write operations
- "plan": Show plan before execution
- "bypassPermissions": Allow all tools (use with caution)

Control Protocol:
- Request: {"subtype": "set_permission_mode", "mode": "..."}
- Response: success or error via control_response
- 60s timeout for request/response

Timing:
- Between turns (after query completes, before next)
- Affects future tool calls immediately
- No effect on in-flight operations

State Changes:
- No rollback on error (previous mode stays)
- No automatic undo mechanism
- Client must handle errors explicitly
- No method to query current mode

Constraints:
- Streaming mode only (requires bidirectional client)
- Must be connected (call Connect() first)
- No SDK-side validation (CLI validates modes)

Use Case: Review code with "default", switch to "acceptEdits" after approval for automated fixes

title: Support Custom Agent Definitions
description:
Add AgentDefinition type with custom prompts, tool restrictions, and model selection (sonnet/opus/haiku/inherit). Pass via CLI options.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:

AgentDefinition Structure:
type AgentDefinition struct {
    Description string   (required - agent purpose)
    Prompt      string   (required - replaces default system prompt)
    Tools       []string (optional - restricts tool access)
    Model       string   (optional - "sonnet"|"opus"|"haiku"|"inherit")
}

Custom Prompts:
- Completely replaces Claude Code default system prompt
- User provides specialized instructions directly
- Example: "You are a code reviewer. Analyze for bugs, performance, security..."

Tool Restrictions:
- Specify exact allowed tools: ["Read", "Grep"]
- If nil/empty, inherits default tool set
- Enables read-only agents, specialized workflows

Model Selection:
- sonnet: Claude 3.5 Sonnet (balanced)
- opus: Claude Opus 4.5 (most capable)
- haiku: Claude 3.5 Haiku (fast/cheap)
- inherit: Use global Options.Model setting

CLI Integration:
- Pass as --agents '{"name": {...}}' JSON
- Handle Windows 8000-char limit (use temp file @filepath)
- Filter nil fields before marshaling

Options Addition:
- Agents map[string]AgentDefinition in Options struct
- WithAgents() option helper

Use Cases: Code reviewers, documentation writers, fast analyzers, security auditors

title: Implement SubagentStop Hook for Background Agent Tracking
description: Specific hook that fires when a subagent/Task tool completes. Critical for tracking background agent lifecycle and coordinating multi-agent workflows.
